summary: High‑energy Long Beach with killer Scarlet>Fire and epic Watchtower
blurb: The show erupts with a scorching Scarlet Begonias > Fire sequence, a marathon The Other One into Morning Dew, and a soaring All‑Along the Watchtower climax. Jerry’s guitar is on manic overdrive while the rhythm section locks tight through He's Gone → Drums and the Space excursion.
review: On November 15, 1987 at Long Beach Arena the Dead delivered one of their most electrifying late‑'80s outings. Set 1 opened with a loose, fun vibe— Iko Iko, Walkin’ Blues, Candyman and a breezy Me & My Uncle → Mexicali Blues segue—before settling into a tight groove on West L.A. Fadeaway, Cassidy and the gentle close of Don’t Ease Me In. The second set kicked off with a blistering Scarlet Begonias > Fire that set the night on fire, then flowed through Looks Like Rain into an unstoppable He's Gone → Drums → Space stretch. What followed was a marathon of The Other One into Morning Dew, spiraling into Around & Around and culminating in an all‑out All Along the Watchtower that left the house shaking. An encore of Black Muddy River provided a soulful coda. Garcia was a “maniac” on guitar—ferocious on The Other One, Watchtower and He’s Gone—while Bob Weir sounded possessed, delivering classic vocal phrasing. Phil Lesh kept the low end rock‑solid despite some audience‑recording distortion in other sources, and the dual drummers created a thrilling Drums → Space journey that powered the set. Brent’s keyboards sit solidly beneath the chaos, completing a night that remains a high‑point of the 1987 run.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings praise the show as excellent, highlighting a stellar Scarlet>Fire, epic Watchtower, and high‑energy jams; positive language appears in three soundboard sources and two audience sources, outweighing mixed comments that focus on recording flaws rather than performance.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crisp, clear mix and no glitches; showcases the full high‑energy performance.

key_highlights: Scarlet Begonias > Fire burns bright early in Set 2
The Other One → Morning Dew → Around & Around → Watchtower marathon
He's Gone → Drums transition delivers a killer rhythmic burst
Black Muddy River encore caps the night with soulful intensity

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia tears through the second set with manic, ferocious guitar work—especially on The Other One and Watchtower—and his vocals hit hard on He’s Gone.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides a rock‑solid low end that anchors the high‑octane jams, keeping the groove tight throughout.
band_performance.Bob: Weir sounds possessed on vocals and rhythm guitar during The Other One, delivering classic phrasing on Looks Like Rain and other numbers.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart’s percussive textures blend with Kreutzmann to create a spacey, driving Drums → Space segment that fuels the second‑set momentum.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann’s steady pulse locks with Hart during the explosive He’s Gone → Drums transition.
